description_long: This object is a solar energy infrastructure installation featuring several large, dark rectangular solar panels mounted on a flat, rectangular base. The panels are arranged in a staggered, multi-level configuration, with some panels tilted at an angle and others lying flat. The base appears to be constructed from a combination of metal and composite materials, with a patterned surface that may provide structural support or facilitate maintenance access. There are small, box-like structures and possible electrical housings distributed around the base, likely for power management or inverter functions. The installation is bordered by low, dark walls or barriers, possibly for safety or containment. The overall design suggests a ground-mounted solar array suitable for urban or industrial environments.
description: A ground-mounted solar array with multiple tilted and flat panels on a patterned base, featuring small electrical housings and bordered by low barriers.
description_view_0: Front view shows the staggered arrangement of solar panels and the patterned base, with electrical housings visible at the rear.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the tilt of the solar panels and the layered structure of the installation.
description_view_2: Rear view reveals the distribution of panels and housings, as well as the full extent of the patterned base.
description_view_3: Opposite side view emphasizes the angular orientation of the panels and the low perimeter barriers.
attribute_panel_orientation: Panels are tilted at approximately 30 degrees, with the tilt direction appearing fixed and consistent across the array.
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted with a metal (likely aluminum or steel) frame and patterned support base.
attribute_array_configuration: Panels are arranged in a staggered, multi-level cluster with approximately 6-8 panels in total.
